title: Introduction to Dart and Flutter
content:
### What is Dart?

Dart is an open-source, general-purpose programming language developed by Google. It is designed for building web, server, desktop, and mobile applications. Dart is a client-optimized language with a C-style syntax that is familiar to developers coming from Java, C#, or JavaScript.

Key features of Dart:

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| Type Safety | Dart is statically typed with a sound type system |
| Null Safety | Null references are handled at compile time |
| Garbage Collection | Automatic memory management |
| JIT and AOT Compilation | Fast development with JIT, fast execution with AOT |
| Concurrent | Supports isolates for parallel execution |

### What is Flutter?

Flutter is Google's UI toolkit for building natively compiled applications for mobile, web, and desktop from a single codebase. Flutter uses Dart as its programming language.

| Platform | Support |
|----------|---------|
| Android | Full support |
| iOS | Full support |
| Web | Full support |
| Windows | Full support |
| macOS | Full support |
| Linux | Full support |

### Why Learn Dart?

Dart is the language behind Flutter, one of the most popular cross-platform frameworks. Learning Dart gives you access to building beautiful, high-performance applications for all major platforms.

> **Key Point:** Dart 3.x introduced record types, pattern matching, and improved class modifiers. It continues to evolve with strong backward compatibility.

quiz.question: What UI toolkit uses Dart as its programming language?
